text:
(1) No person shall authorize or knowingly permit a motor vehicle owned by such person or under such person’s hire or control to be driven upon any highway by any person who has not been issued a currently valid driver’s or minor driver’s license or an instruction permit or shall cause or knowingly permit such person to drive a motor vehicle upon any highway in violation of the conditions, limitations, or restrictions contained in a license or permit which has been issued to such other person.

(2) Any person who violates any provision of this section commits a class B traffic infraction.
markdown:
## Title 42
#### Article 2. Drivers’ Licenses
##### § 42-2-140 — Permitting unauthorized person to drive

(1) No person shall authorize or knowingly permit a motor vehicle owned by such person or under such person’s hire or control to be driven upon any highway by any person who has not been issued a currently valid driver’s or minor driver’s license or an instruction permit or shall cause or knowingly permit such person to drive a motor vehicle upon any highway in violation of the conditions, limitations, or restrictions contained in a license or permit which has been issued to such other person.

(2) Any person who violates any provision of this section commits a class B traffic infraction.

subsections.text: No person shall authorize or knowingly permit a motor vehicle owned by such person or under such person’s hire or control to be driven upon any highway by any person who has not been issued a currently valid driver’s or minor driver’s license or an instruction permit or shall cause or knowingly permit such person to drive a motor vehicle upon any highway in violation of the conditions, limitations, or restrictions contained in a license or permit which has been issued to such other person.
Any person who violates any provision of this section commits a class B traffic infraction.
